seo_title: Improving Agent Robustness via Noisy Environments
meta_description: Learning to Act under Noise: Enhancing Agent Robustness via Noisy Environments explores the gap between idealized training settings and real-world interaction…
body_html:
Direct answer: Despite strong performance on existing benchmarks, LLMs often exhibit notable degradation when deployed in real-world settings due to a fundamental mismatch between idealized training settings and real-world interaction dynamics.

NoisyAgent explicitly incorporates environmental imperfections into the agent learning process, addressing the gap between idealized training settings and real-world interaction dynamics. The framework introduces perturbations into the training pipeline to simulate user noise and tool noise, progressively increasing difficulty as the model adapts to the current noise level.

Extensive Experiments Consistently improves agent robustness under noisy and dynamic environments.

Idealized Benchmarks Yields performance gains, suggesting controlled exposure to environmental noise promotes more generalizable reasoning and decision-making behaviors.

Analysis and Findings Our analysis reveals that training under noise conditions also yields performance gains on idealized benchmarks, suggesting that controlled exposure to environmental noise promotes more generalizable reasoning and decision-making behaviors.

source.title: Learning to Act under Noise: Enhancing Agent Robustness via Noisy Environments
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called NoisyAgent to improve the robustness of large language models (LLMs) when deployed in real-world settings with noisy environments. The framework introduces environmental imperfections into the agent learning process, including user noise and tool noise, which are simulated during training. This approach has been shown to consistently improve agent performance under challenging conditions, while also promoting more generalizable reasoning and decision-making behaviors on idealized benchmarks.
